The bioactive constituents of Ruellia oblongifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions.
